rows.source_ref: The £10K Gamble - [The Journey], trade-journal thread by user The_Baller (post #63), forums.babypips.com, 2019. https://forums.babypips.com/t/the-10k-gamble-the-journey/214330
rows.concept: Enter in the trend direction when the Ichimoku Chikou Span (lagging line) pierces completely through the Kumo cloud, using only Chikou plus the cloud with Tenkan/Kijun removed.
rows.eligibility_reason: Entry is a deterministic standard-Ichimoku signal implementable in pure MQL5; exit/stop/TP are left to the trader so we would define our own within the framework.
rows.notes: Author frames it as a beginner trend strategy on H4, S/R plotted off the daily (post #65). No exit, stop or position-size rules given. Generic trend/breakout with low orthogonality vs existing index/metal trend variants; the 'Chikou pierces the cloud completely' trigger needs a precise definition (lagging line clearing both Senkou spans).

rows.source_ref: The £10K Gamble - [The Journey], trade-journal thread by user The_Baller (post #16 steps; posts #79-80), forums.babypips.com, 2019. https://forums.babypips.com/t/the-10k-gamble-the-journey/214330
rows.strategy_name: Weekly S/R Swing 'Follow the Big Players'
rows.concept: Discretionary swing trading: hand-draw support/resistance on the monthly then the weekly chart within a trend, enter 'somewhere' at a zone, and set a very wide stop out of the noise.
rows.eligibility_reason: No mechanical rules: subjective hand-drawn S/R zones, 'you'll find an entry in there somewhere', Chikou Span used only as a visual S/R marker — no deterministic entry/exit trigger to implement.
rows.notes: Thread's headline method (post #16, page 6). Traded off the weekly chart with monthly-trend context (W1 not in the testable enum; D1-native is the closest proxy). Stated risk 5-10% per trade (above DXZ/FTMO caps, though we set our own). Entirely discretionary; not mechanizable.

notes: Text extracted with pdfplumber (Read tool could not render — poppler missing); text layer is clean.
Thread is mostly forum chatter/banter; two rule statements found: post #16 (discretionary weekly S/R) and post #63 (Ichimoku Chikou-cloud breakout).
Only the Ichimoku breakout has a deterministic entry; the headline S/R method is discretionary and rejected.
